title: claude-mem implementation teardown: zero verification confirmed; 5 mechanics worth adopting
summary: Deep teardown of thedotmack/claude mem v13.5.6 ~66k LOC TS, clone studied 2026 06 12 . CONFIRMED zero memory verification: no staleness checks against code, observations' files read/files modified stored as JSON strings
body: Deep teardown of thedotmack/claude-mem v13.5.6 (~66k LOC TS, clone studied 2026-06-12). CONFIRMED zero memory verification: no staleness checks against code, observations' files_read/files_modified stored as JSON strings never re-resolved, no citation checking, no supersede logic — their 'stale' code is all process hygiene. Their capture: PostToolUse hooks -> durable SQLite queue -> persistent Claude Agent SDK observer session (burns the USER's Claude quota) that emits XML observations. Their '95% token savings' is real math: discovery_tokens (cumulative SDK tokens spent producing each observation) minus read-cost of the compressed observation. Recall: SessionStart timeline index (~800 tokens, one-liners + full detail for newest K), 3-layer search->timeline->get_observations MCP workflow taught via a no-op '__IMPORTANT' tool description, PLUS PreToolUse(Read) hook injecting file-relevant observations at read time. Weaknesses: Node+Bun+Python/uv+daemon+chroma sprawl, observer quota burns, half-finished v2 server. ADOPT (ranked): 1) discovery_tokens per packet -> 'this packet saved N tokens' receipts; 2) PreToolUse(Read) injection of VERIFIED packets at file-read moment (our code graph makes it strictly better); 3) workflow-teaching pseudo-tool; 4) timeline-as-index injection format; 5) SSE live viewer feed. Positioning: they optimize capture volume + visible savings, no answer to 'is it still true' — pair their value mechanics with our verification.
